title: How to get a police certificate – Seychelles - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate – Seychelles
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.
Do I need to provide a Police Certificate with my Application for Permanent Residence? Yes
Name of document(s) to get: Character Certificate

## How to get a Police Certificate:

### If you are a citizen of Seychelles:

#### If you live in Seychelles:
You should apply in person to the Commissioner of Police (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must provide:
- Identification documents: birth certificate or passport
- Must allow fingerprints to be taken

#### If you live outside of Seychelles:
You should apply in writing to the Commissioner of Police (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must provide:
- Copies of your birth certificate and passport
- The address where you lived while in Seychelles
- Date you left Seychelles

### If you are not a citizen of Seychelles:

#### If you live in Seychelles:
You should apply in person to the Commissioner of Police (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must provide:
- Copies of your passport and proof of legal stay in Seychelles, visa/permit
- Copy of your birth certificate
- Must allow fingerprints to be taken

#### If you live outside of Seychelles:
You should apply in writing to the Commissioner of Police (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must provide:
- Copies of your birth certificate and passport
- The address where you lived in Seychelles
- Date you left Seychelles

### Information and documents you need:
- All information and documentation needed are explained above.

### Special notes and considerations
Commissioner of Police Police Headquarters P.O. Box 46 Victoria, Mahé Seychelles
Telephone: +248-4-28 80 00 Fax: +248 4 32 20 05 E-mail: compol@seypolice.sc
